**Framework Note:** This concept's content is fully covered by the Holonic Venture Decomposition Framework (JOS-FW-001), §10 (Three Levels of Utility). This object is retained as a teaching entry point and historical record.

# Three Levels of Utility

## Explains

Information systems progress through three stages — describing what IS,
prescribing what SHOULD BE, generating what's MISSING — and most stop too early.

## The Concept

Level 1 (Descriptive): "Here's what this venture has." Audit, survey, inventory.
This is the baseline — knowing what exists. Most governance systems stop here
because description is safe. It doesn't require judgment, doesn't create
obligations, doesn't generate work. It just observes.

Level 2 (Prescriptive): "Here's what it SHOULD have." Standards, templates, what
does good look like (WDGLL). This level adds judgment to description. It requires
someone to define the standard, which means taking a position and defending it.
Many governance systems reach Level 2 but treat it as the endpoint — standards
exist but nobody enforces or generates from them.

Level 3 (Generative): "Here's every slot, here's what's missing, here's how to
fill them." The system actively produces work, not just assessments. It identifies
gaps, creates tasks, and in some cases generates draft content to fill those gaps.
This is governance as engine, not governance as auditor.

The DNA analogy illuminates the progression: mapping the genome (descriptive) →
understanding disease markers (prescriptive) → editing genes to fix problems
(generative). Each level unlocks the next. JOS aims for Level 3.

## Why This Matters for Governance

Most governance frameworks are permanently stuck at Level 1 or 2. They can tell
you what you have and what you should have, but they cannot actively drive
creation of what's missing. JOS's ambition is Level 3 — a system that generates
governance objects, identifies gaps, and produces actionable work queues.

This concept explains WHY JOS invests in templates, slot definitions, and
automation targets. These aren't overhead — they're the infrastructure required
to reach Level 3 utility. Without them, JOS would be just another audit
framework.

## Connects To

- JOS-C40 (Generative Absence)
- JOS-C17 (Graduated Automation)
- Framework §10 (utility levels)
